data.name: zone_dict
data.description: Dictionary mapping zone_id (TAZ ID) to zone infrastructure information. Each zone contains references to all infrastructure elements within its boundaries including lanes, intersections, highways, ramps, and transit stations. Now includes zone subgraphs (derived from Foundation Layer) and boundary information.

zone_id.shape: List of (x, y) coordinate tuples defining zone polygon boundary
zone_id.edges: List of all edge IDs within the zone
zone_id.lanes: List of all lane IDs within the zone
zone_id.intersections: List of traffic light controlled intersection/junction IDs
zone_id.ramps: List of ramp TLS (traffic light system) IDs
zone_id.subway_stations: List of subway station IDs in this zone
zone_id.bus_stops: List of bus stop IDs in this zone

subgraphs.lane_subgraph: NetworkX DiGraph - zone's lane connectivity subgraph (from Foundation Layer)
subgraphs.road_subgraph: NetworkX DiGraph - zone's road connectivity subgraph (from Foundation Layer)
subgraphs.transit_subgraph: NetworkX DiGraph - zone's transit subgraph (stations + routes)

boundary.entry_roads: List of road IDs that enter this zone from outside
boundary.exit_roads: List of road IDs that exit this zone to outside
boundary.boundary_intersections: List of intersection IDs at zone boundaries

stats.total_highway_length_m: float - total highway length in meters
stats.total_bus_stops: int - total number of bus stops
stats.total_subway_stations: int - total number of subway stations

data.usage_example:
```python
# Access zone infrastructure
zone_id = '2_5'
if zone_id in zone_dict:
    zone_info = zone_dict[zone_id]
    print(f"Zone {zone_id} has {zone_info['stats']['total_lanes']} lanes")
    print(f"Intersections: {zone_info['intersections']}")
    print(f"Bus stops: {zone_info['bus_stops']}")

    # Access zone subgraphs (from Foundation Layer)
    if 'subgraphs' in zone_info:
        lane_subgraph = zone_info['subgraphs']['lane_subgraph']
        if lane_subgraph:
            print(f"Zone lane subgraph has {len(lane_subgraph.nodes())} lanes")

    # Access boundary information
    if 'boundary' in zone_info:
        entry_roads = zone_info['boundary']['entry_roads']
        exit_roads = zone_info['boundary']['exit_roads']
        print(f"Entry roads: {entry_roads}")
        print(f"Exit roads: {exit_roads}")

# Find all zones with highways
highway_zones = [zid for zid, info in zone_dict.items() if info['highway_edges']]
print(f"Zones with highways: {highway_zones}")

# Find zones with transit stations
transit_zones = [zid for zid, info in zone_dict.items() 
                 if info['subway_stations'] or info['bus_stops']]

# Get all lanes in a zone
zone_lanes = zone_dict[zone_id]['lanes']
```

data.name: zone_graph
data.description: NetworkX DiGraph representing zone adjacency relationships. Nodes are zones, edges connect adjacent zones that share boundary junctions or edges. Enhanced with boundary road and capacity information.

node_attributes.edge_count: int - number of edges in the zone
node_attributes.has_highway: bool - whether zone contains highway edges
node_attributes.has_ramp: bool - whether zone contains ramp TLS
node_attributes.has_transit: bool - whether zone has subway stations or bus stops

edge_attributes.shared_junctions: List of junction IDs shared between zones
edge_attributes.shared_edges: List of edge IDs connecting the zones
edge_attributes.boundary_roads: List of road IDs that cross the zone boundary
edge_attributes.boundary_capacity: float - total capacity of boundary roads (veh/hr estimate)

data.usage_example:
```python
# Find adjacent zones
zone_id = '2_5'
adjacent_zones = list(zone_graph.successors(zone_id))
print(f"Zones adjacent to {zone_id}: {adjacent_zones}")

# Get zone attributes
for node, attrs in zone_graph.nodes(data=True):
    if attrs.get('has_highway'):
        print(f"Zone {node} has highways")

# Find shared boundaries between zones
for u, v, data in zone_graph.edges(data=True):
    shared = data.get('shared_junctions', [])
    boundary_roads = data.get('boundary_roads', [])
    capacity = data.get('boundary_capacity', 0)
    print(f"{u} <-> {v}: {len(shared)} shared junctions, {len(boundary_roads)} boundary roads, capacity={capacity} veh/hr")

# Find all zones with transit
transit_zones = [n for n, d in zone_graph.nodes(data=True) if d.get('has_transit')]
```

data.name: network_graphs
data.description: Foundation Layer (Layer 1) - Complete network graphs covering ALL infrastructure in the network. These are the authoritative source from which zone subgraphs and module-specific graphs are derived.

lane_graph.description: Complete lane connectivity graph (all non-internal lanes)
lane_graph.nodes: lane_id (str) - all lanes in the network

node_attributes.zone_id: str | None - zone containing this lane

edge_attributes.junction_id: str | None - junction ID for via_junction connections
edge_attributes.via_internal_lane: str | None - internal lane used for connection

road_graph.nodes: road_id (str) - all roads in the network

edge_attributes.is_signalized: bool - whether junction has traffic light

transit_graph.description: Complete transit network graph (all routes and stations)

data.usage_example:
```python
# Access Foundation Layer graphs
lane_graph = network_graphs['lane_graph']
road_graph = network_graphs['road_graph']
transit_graph = network_graphs['transit_graph']

# Get all highway roads in the network
highway_roads = [
    road_id for road_id in road_graph.nodes()
    if road_graph.nodes[road_id].get('road_category') == 'highway'
]
print(f"Total highway roads: {len(highway_roads)}")

# Get all highway lanes
highway_lanes = [
    lane_id for lane_id in lane_graph.nodes()
    if lane_graph.nodes[lane_id].get('lane_type') == 'highway'
]
print(f"Total highway lanes: {len(highway_lanes)}")

# Find lane's zone
lane_id = 'road_1_0'
if lane_id in lane_graph.nodes():
    zone_id = lane_graph.nodes[lane_id].get('zone_id')
    print(f"Lane {lane_id} is in zone {zone_id}")
```

data.name: network_dicts
data.description: Foundation Layer (Layer 1) - Complete metadata dictionaries covering ALL infrastructure in the network. More detailed than graph node attributes.

value.lane_index: int - lane index on the road
value.zone_id: str | None - zone containing this lane
value.lane_group: str | None - lane group ID for intersection lanes
value.connected_intersections: List[str] - intersections this lane connects to
value.controlled_by_ramp: str | None - ramp ID controlling this lane

value.lanes: List[str] - lane IDs for this road

value.lane_id: str - lane where station is located

data.usage_example:
```python
# Access Foundation Layer dictionaries
lane_dict = network_dicts['lane_dict']
road_dict = network_dicts['road_dict']
station_dict = network_dicts['station_dict']

# Find all lanes controlled by ramps
ramp_controlled_lanes = [
    lane_id for lane_id, info in lane_dict.items()
    if info.get('controlled_by_ramp')
]
print(f"Lanes controlled by ramps: {len(ramp_controlled_lanes)}")

# Find all highway roads
highway_roads = [
    road_id for road_id, info in road_dict.items()
    if info.get('road_category') == 'highway'
]
print(f"Highway roads: {len(highway_roads)}")

# Get zone for a lane
lane_id = 'road_1_0'
if lane_id in lane_dict:
    zone_id = lane_dict[lane_id].get('zone_id')
    direction = lane_dict[lane_id].get('direction')
    print(f"Lane {lane_id}: zone={zone_id}, direction={direction}")
```

data.name: transit_graph
data.description: NetworkX DiGraph representing the public transit network including bus and subway routes and stations. Nodes are either routes or stations, edges represent service relationships.

nodes.station_id: Bus stop or subway station identifier

node_attributes.zone_id: Zone containing the station (for station nodes)
node_attributes.lane_id: Lane where station is located (for station nodes)

edge_attributes.stop_index: int - position of station on route (for 'serves' edges)

data.usage_example:
```python
# Find all stations served by a route
route_id = 'route_bus1_eastbound'
stations = [n for n in transit_graph.successors(route_id) 
            if transit_graph.nodes[n].get('node_type') == 'station']
print(f"Stations on {route_id}: {stations}")

# Find routes serving a station
station_id = 'stop_bus1_0_east'
routes = [n for n in transit_graph.predecessors(station_id)
          if transit_graph.nodes[n].get('node_type') == 'route']
print(f"Routes at {station_id}: {routes}")

# Get stations in order for a route
stations_ordered = []
for n in transit_graph.successors(route_id):
    edge_data = transit_graph.edges[route_id, n]
    if edge_data.get('edge_type') == 'serves':
        stations_ordered.append((edge_data['stop_index'], n))
stations_ordered.sort()
print([s[1] for s in stations_ordered])

# Find transfer stations (multiple routes)
for node, attrs in transit_graph.nodes(data=True):
    if attrs.get('node_type') == 'station':
        serving_routes = list(transit_graph.predecessors(node))
        if len(serving_routes) > 1:
            print(f"Transfer station {node}: {serving_routes}")
```

data.name: bus_route_info
data.description: Dictionary containing detailed bus route information including static structure and real-time operational data. Updated during simulation runs.

route_id.edges: List[str] - ordered list of edge IDs along the route
route_id.total_length_m: float - total route length in meters
route_id.scheduled_runtime_s: float - planned end-to-end runtime in seconds

stations.position_m: float - position along route in meters

segments.intersections_count: int - number of intersections in segment

route_id.zones_served: List[str] - list of zone IDs the route passes through

transfer_stations.connecting_routes: List[str] - other bus routes at this station
transfer_stations.connecting_subway_lines: List[str] - subway lines at this station

realtime.active_buses: List[str] - vehicle IDs currently in service
realtime.last_update_step: int - simulation step of last update

data.usage_example:
```python
# Get route information
route_id = 'route_bus1_eastbound'
if route_id in bus_route_info:
    route = bus_route_info[route_id]
    print(f"Route {route['route_name']}: {route['total_length_m']:.0f}m")
    print(f"Direction: {route['direction']}")
    print(f"Zones served: {route['zones_served']}")
    
    # List stations
    for station in route['stations']:
        print(f"  Stop {station['stop_index']}: {station['station_id']} (Zone {station['zone_id']})")
    
    # Check real-time data
    rt = route['realtime']
    print(f"Active buses: {rt['bus_count']}")
    print(f"Current headway: {rt['current_headway_s']}s")
    
    # Find delayed segments
    for seg in rt['segment_status']:
        if seg['delay_s'] > 60:
            print(f"Delay on {seg['from_station']} -> {seg['to_station']}: {seg['delay_s']}s")

# Find routes through a specific zone
target_zone = '2_5'
routes_in_zone = [rid for rid, info in bus_route_info.items() 
                  if target_zone in info['zones_served']]
print(f"Routes through zone {target_zone}: {routes_in_zone}")
```

functions.name: get_zone_infrastructure
functions.description: Query infrastructure for a specific zone. Returns filtered infrastructure information based on the requested type.

output_format.returns: Filtered zone information based on infra_type, or error dict if zone not found

functions.usage_example:
```python
# This function is available through zone_dict directly
# Get all infrastructure for a zone
zone_info = zone_dict.get('2_5', {})

# Get specific infrastructure types by filtering
zone_id = '2_5'
if zone_id in zone_dict:
    # Lanes only
    lanes = zone_dict[zone_id]['lanes']
    
    # Intersections only
    intersections = zone_dict[zone_id]['intersections']
    
    # Highways only
    highway_info = {
        'highway_segments': zone_dict[zone_id]['highway_segments'],
        'highway_edges': zone_dict[zone_id]['highway_edges']
    }
    
    # Transit only
    transit_info = {
        'subway_stations': zone_dict[zone_id]['subway_stations'],
        'bus_stops': zone_dict[zone_id]['bus_stops']
    }
```

functions.name: get_zones_by_infrastructure
functions.description: Find zones containing a specific infrastructure element.

output_format.returns: List of zone IDs containing the infrastructure element
